channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Pakistani Advisory Member Joins King’s Trust International to Support Youth Development
item.description: Businesswoman Huma Fakhar expressed her honour at returning to Buckingham Palace as an advisory member representing Pakistan for The King's Trust International. She described meeting global leaders an...

item.title: Ann Margret Shines in Behind-the-Scenes Photos from 'Murderers’ Row' (1966)
item.description: During the filming of the 1966 spy-spoof 'Murderers’ Row,' Ann Margret emerged as a defining figure of 1960s Hollywood glamour. These portraits capture her portraying Suzie with a vibrant, playful ene...

item.title: Analysis of Tanzania Banking Sector Performance in 2025: Assets and Profits Grow, CRDB and NMB Lead Market
item.description: In 2025, Tanzania's banking sector recorded strong growth, with total assets reaching TZS 79.4 trillion and net profits after tax increasing by 21% to TZS 2.62 trillion, according to unaudited results...

item.title: Nova Scotia Fuel Prices Increase Following Middle East Tensions
item.description: Residents of Nova Scotia are facing higher fuel costs ahead of the upcoming long weekend. After a temporary decline last week, the Nova Scotia Energy Board raised gasoline and diesel prices. In Halifa...

item.title: Oxford Economics: African governments must navigate complex choices amid global powers' competition for influence
item.description: According to Oxford Economics, African governments are facing increasingly difficult decisions to safeguard their sovereignty and strategic interests as major global powers ramp up competition for inf...

item.title: Preview of AIPAD Photography Show Featuring Iconic and Contemporary Works in New York
item.description: The Photography Show, organized by the Association of International Photography Art Dealers (AIPAD), is scheduled to take place from April 22 to 26, 2026, at the Park Avenue Armory in New York City. T...

item.title: Madlanga Commission Dismisses Brown Mogotsi’s Application to Recuse Evidence Leader Matthew Chaskalson
item.description: The Madlanga commission has dismissed an application by North West businessman Brown Mogotsi to recuse evidence leader Advocate Matthew Chaskalson. On Friday, commission chairperson Judge Mbuyiseli Ma...

item.title: Grocery Prices in South Africa Have Soared Over the Past Five Years
item.description: Supermarket prices across Johannesburg and surrounding areas have surged dramatically over the past five years, with many essential items costing between 70% and 100% more than they did in 2021. For e...

item.title: CBS Anchor Faces Challenges Covering Trump's China Visit Amid Visa Issues and Broadcast Restrictions
item.description: CBS Evening News anchor Tony Dokoupil encountered multiple obstacles while reporting on President Donald Trump's visit to China. Unable to secure a Chinese visa, Dokoupil reported from Taiwan, over 1,...

item.title: Court fines plaintiff N1m for delaying Jonathan’s 2027 presidential eligibility case
item.description: A Federal High Court for Abuja don fine one plaintiff, Johnmary Jideobi, N1 million because e dey delay prosecution of im case wey aim to stop former President Goodluck Jonathan from running for 2027 ...

item.title: Alleged insurance murder syndicate used payouts for luxury wedding and helicopter, court hears
item.description: In the Polokwane magistrate’s court, an investigating officer testified that an alleged insurance murder syndicate used proceeds from their schemes to fund a lavish wedding complete with a helicopter....

item.title: University of Toronto Revokes Buffy Sainte-Marie’s Honorary Degree Amid Controversy
item.description: The University of Toronto has formally revoked the honorary degree previously awarded to Indigenous singer-songwriter and activist Buffy Sainte-Marie. The decision comes after scrutiny over statements...

item.title: Judge Rules Jacob Zuma Arms Deal Trial Must Proceed Without Further Delay
item.description: In a significant ruling, Judge Nkosinathi Chili of the KwaZulu-Natal Division of the High Court in Pietermaritzburg has ordered that the long-running arms deal corruption case against former President...

item.title: Trump Concludes Beijing Visit with Limited Trade Success and Discussions on Taiwan and Iran
item.description: US President Donald Trump ended his visit to China on May 15, 2026, without major breakthroughs in trade or assistance regarding the Iran conflict, despite spending two days praising Chinese President...

item.title: Canada Eligible to Participate in Eurovision Song Contest Pending Formal Invitation
item.description: Canada could potentially participate in a future Eurovision Song Contest, but no official entry has yet been proposed, according to contest director Martin Green. For Canada to compete, its national b...

item.title: Fuel Prices Go Up, Kenyans Brace for Higher Transport and Living Costs
item.description: Kenyans are feeling the pinch after a significant increase in fuel prices that took effect from midnight. Super Petrol has gone up by Sh16.65 per litre, bringing the new price to Sh214.25, while Diese...

item.title: CAAP Holds Search and Rescue Exercise in Manila Bay to Boost Emergency Preparedness
item.description: The Civil Aviation Authority of the Philippines (CAAP) successfully carried out a search and rescue exercise, known as SAREX, at the dolomite beach along Roxas Boulevard in Manila on May 15, 2026. Thi...

item.title: Harvey Norman current catalogue highlights: Top tech, audio and home deals
item.description: Harvey Norman is currently running a range of attractive discounts across electronics, audio equipment, televisions, laptops and home furniture as part of its latest catalogue sales. Standout offers i...

item.title: Lagos State Food Hubs Achieve ₦3.8 Billion in Transactions as Government Expands Agro Markets
item.description: The Lagos State Government don reveal say their food hub initiative don record over ₦3.8 billion in transactions. According to the Agric Commissioner, Abisola Olusanya, the Idi-Oro Fresh Food Hub for ...

item.title: Maraga, Sifuna and United Opposition agree on one candidate to challenge President Ruto in 2027
item.description: A group of opposition leaders, led by former Chief Justice David Maraga and Nairobi senator Edwin Sifuna, have come together under the United Opposition banner to push for a single presidential candid...
